type: DEFER
files: tiktoken/core.py
spec: Add a 'transformers' keyword-only parameter to Encoding.encode() (and ideally encode_ordinary/encode_to_numpy if trivial, but encode() is the required one). Signature addition: transformers: Callable[[str], str] | Sequence[Callable[[str], str]] | None = None. Behavior: if transformers is provided, apply it to  BEFORE any special-token checks (disallowed_special regex search) and before passing to self._core_bpe.encode. If a single callable is given, apply it once: text = transformers(text). If a sequence/list of callables is given, apply them in order, each receiving the previous one's output: for fn in transformers: text = fn(text). If None (default), skip transformation entirely (no behavior change, so existing callers unaffected). Each transformer must accept a str and return a str; no special validation needed beyond that (let TypeErrors propagate naturally if misused). Apply transformation before the UnicodeEncodeError fallback re-encoding logic already in encode() — i.e. transform first, then proceed with existing allowed_special/disallowed_special logic on the transformed text. Needed import: typing.Callable (add to the typing import list at top of core.py). No changes needed to the Rust core (_tiktoken.CoreBPE) — this is pure Python preprocessing.
note: transformers param for encode()

type: CLAIM
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Acknowledged, implementing your transformers spec (Callable[[str],str] | Sequence[...] | None, applied before disallowed_special check) plus my return_positions param in encode(). Will send DONE when finished.

type: DONE
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Implemented return_positions=True and your transformers param in Encoding.encode(). Both tested independently and combined; existing test suite (32 tests) passes.
